title: Experiment temporary 240 FPS TitleNativeJobTiming during autoload
description: User idea: test whether raising the title/native job frame-rate constant from the current 60Hz-derived TitleNativeJobTiming frame rate to ~240Hz only during the pre-world/autoload path can speed character loading. Scope: make it gated/temporary, restore normal timing after the character/world load is complete, and validate with the full runtime oracle so this does not hide failures or overfit. Investigate whether this changes frame_delta-driven menu/title/load pumps safely versus causing physics/timing side effects.
close_reason: Informal 240-FPS nudge showed no meaningful speedup; keep FrameRate at 60

title: Prove DLL character load is correct + faster than native menu (vanilla offline)
description: New goal: with er_effects_rs.dll hooked into vanilla ER offline, drive a COMPLETE, CORRECT character load via the DLL (in-context functor invokes allowed -- the zero-input purity bar is dropped) and PROVE it is (a) correct (same char/map/state as a native load) and (b) faster than normal menu navigation. Implement STAGE 2 of own_stepper (invoke d180 Load-Game functor -> ProfileLoadDialog -> load_activate slot N -> native pump mounts c30=real+ac0+char via io18/io20 -> continue_confirm -> SetState(5)). Add frame-stamped timing + correctness instrumentation. Capture native baseline. Document for Ghidra. Save-safety: never SetState(5) until c30 real-mounted.

description: Create a helper/automation path for driving Elden Ring menu/test flows and capturing structured evidence instead of relying on LLM/OCR-guided clicking. It may use hooks/memory reads where needed for branching state.

comments.author: Banon
comments.text:
Pivot task list:
1. Stabilize hook-side telemetry: make player availability, menu/load phase, selected call states, and last driver command visible in structured JSON without render-path false negatives.
2. Add a driver command channel: let the Linux helper request apply/remove/toggle actions through a small file or named pipe that the injected DLL consumes, so proof toggles do not depend on ImGui clicks.
3. Investigate direct-load/continue entrypoint: inspect fromsoftware-rs/debug symbols and local mod references for a safe game-side continue/load-latest trigger; prefer calling a known function over menu navigation.
4. Implement bounded launcher flow: launch with LazyLoader, wait for telemetry phases, send only sparse prompt-dismiss nudges if direct-load is unavailable, and fail with artifacts instead of mashing.
5. Validate checkbox/menu behavior separately: once loaded, prove overlay checkbox on/off semantics with screenshots and telemetry; if command-channel toggles pass but UI clicks fail, isolate hudhook input/coordinate handling.
6. Record evidence and cleanup: capture before/on/off PNGs, telemetry JSON, chains-debug.log, and driver logs; stop Elden Ring cleanly and leave a concise Beads handoff.

Direct Proton launch preserves the overlay/DLL and reaches saved-character loading without Seamless prompts.

- Stabilized telemetry via ER_EFFECTS_TELEMETRY_PATH / er-effects-telemetry.json: player_available, current_animation_id, network_sync, last_driver_command, and call enabled/active/apply_failed states.
- Direct game-load entrypoint remains not identified in local fromsoftware-rs RVAs or strings; no safe known function to call yet. For now, direct Proton launch + sparse nudges is the safer closed path.
- Validation artifact: target/smoke/driver-direct-command-20260612-085432. The driver reached player_available=true and command-channel set 0 on/off succeeded; final telemetry records last_driver_command="ok: set 0 off" and call 0 inactive.

title: Overlay can't render: hudhook 0.9.1 DX12 command-queue detection fails on ER 1.16.1
description: In-game smoke test (er-offline.exe, DLL auto-injected via dllMods/LazyLoader) showed the DLL injects and runs stably, but the ImGui overlay never appears. chains-debug.log spams every frame: 'hudhook::hooks::dx12: Couldn't find command queue pointer in swap chain struct (512 out of 512 pointers were readable)' followed by 'Initialization context incomplete' and 'Render error: HRESULT(0xFFFFFFFF)'. Reproduced with ALL other mods disabled (only er_effects_rs.dll loaded), so it is not a mod conflict. The pinned hudhook is commit 6a361de (0.9.1); its DX12 hook scans the swapchain struct for the D3D12 command queue pointer and never finds it on this game build/GPU, so the renderer context never completes. Root cause is upstream in hudhook, shared via the fromsoftware-rs 'debug' crate (both it and this DLL depend on the same hudhook git rev, so they must be bumped together).
design: Fix options, in order of preference: (1) Bump hudhook to a version whose DX12 hook obtains the command queue by hooking ID3D12CommandQueue::ExecuteCommandLists instead of scanning the swapchain (later hudhook reworked this); requires coordinating the bump in fromsoftware-rs since the debug crate pins the same rev, and adapting to any ImguiRenderLoop API changes. (2) If staying on 0.9.x, investigate passing the command queue explicitly. (3) Confirm whether a DX11-on-12 path is viable. Note: ProDebug._dll in dllMods is a native (non-hudhook) tool, so it is not a usable comparison.
acceptance_criteria: Overlay renders in-game on ER 1.16.1; chains-debug.log shows a 'Found command queue pointer ... at offset' line and no 'Initialization context incomplete' spam.
close_reason: Fixed for this repo via Banon-Labs/hudhook er-proton-cursor-poll branch: DX12 fallback renders overlay in ER, and input follow-up verified clicks work. Upstream fallback PR opened as veeenu/hudhook#257.

description:
## Summary

`vtable-rs 0.1.4` is already in `Cargo.lock` (transitive via `eldenring` + `fromsoftware-shared`) and is used heavily upstream (`#[vtable]` + `VPtr<dyn ..Vmt, Self>`). This repo's own code uses it **zero** times — the title/menu/dialog autoload flow in `src/experiments.rs` does all C++ virtual interaction by hand with `safe_read_usize` + `std::mem::transmute` + `*_VTABLE_RVA` constants.

Read-only audit (2026-06-19). Scripts: `/tmp/vt_audit.py`, `/tmp/classify_vt.py`.

## Missed opportunities (vtable-rs territory only)

- **19 distinct game vtables** modeled as bare `*_VTABLE_RVA` consts -> each a candidate `#[vtable]` trait:
  FUNCTOR, ITEM, MEMBERFUNCJOB, MENU_ITEM_LOADGAME_FUNCTOR, MENU_WINDOW_JOB, MENU_WINDOW_PROXY, MENU_WINDOW, MOVIE, MSGBOX_DIALOG, OWNER_OBJ, PROFILE_LOAD_DIALOG, RESMGR_EXPECTED, ROUTER_THIS, ROUTER, SCENE_OBJ_PROXY, SELECTOR_STEP, TITLE_FLOW_CONTEXT, TITLE_OWNER, TITLE_TOP_DIALOG.
- **36 manual vtable-pointer reads** (`let vt = safe_read_usize(obj)` == `obj->vftable`).
- **10 manual virtual-method dispatch sites** (`safe_read_usize(vtable + SLOT)` -> transmute -> call): experiments.rs lines 1310, 1378, 1415, 1518, 1746, 1759, 2544, 2721, 2928, 7713.
- **14 vtable-identity / RTTI checks** (`vt == base + X_VTABLE_RVA`): experiments.rs 785, 817, 2503, 3817, 4843, 5429, 5478, 6117, 7644, 8009, 8361, 8367; telemetry.rs 490, 502. These could fold into `fromsoftware-shared` `rtti.rs`/`subclass.rs`.

**Total: ~19 vtable types / 60 raw call+check sites** would become typed `VPtr` access, typed virtual calls, and typed downcasts.

## Explicitly NOT in scope (don't inflate the figure)

vtable-rs does not address these (different tool — RVA bindings / detours):
- ~47 `transmute(base + RVA)` free/static game-function calls.
- ~22 `transmute(original)` MinHook trampolines (hooks.rs, crashlog.rs, input hooks).
- 19 `transmute(game_rva(..))` RVA calls in `er-save-loader`.

## Cost / why low-risk

Crate already compiled in; upstream already demonstrates the pattern for FromSoftware objects. Refactor is mechanical per type: declare `#[vtable] trait XVmt`, define `#[repr(C)]` struct with `VPtr`, replace the scattered raw reads. Broader `safe_read_usize` footprint is 240 sites; the ~60 here are the subset where a typed vtable removes transmute + offset math and adds compile-time slot-layout safety.

title: Generalize the menu-item Update hook into a reusable menu->function resolver
description: The cap_menu_item_update_hook (0x1407ad1c0 MenuWindowJob::Update) + functor_chain_hits_factory + decode_thunk_hop built for autoload (er-effects-rs-av4) is a GENERAL tool: it resolves any FD4 menu item to the native function it invokes by decoding the std::function _Do_call thunk chain at [item+0xa8] (vtable+0x10 -> add rcx,8; jmp <lambda/thunk> ... -> real fn). This defeats the std::function indirection that dead-ends static rel32 tracing (the 'orphan thunk' wall, e.g. dialog_factory 0x14081ead0 had 0 static callers but resolves trivially at runtime via this hook). Reusable to map ANY obfuscated 'menu action -> game function' binding: hook the menu-item Update, let the native pump tick items, log each item's vtable + functor target + caller stack. FUTURE WORK (user, 2026-06-16: 'TONS of menu interactions that obfuscate game functions'): (1) extract the hook+decoder into a standalone read-only menu-RE helper (target-by-RVA + functor-target filter); (2) optionally hook the iterator 0x1407aa1f0 (Sequence::Update) to enumerate whole menu trees; (3) record a catalog of menu-item -> native-function mappings. Defer until the autoload (av4) lands.

title: Cupcake env guard false positive on heredoc evidence text
description:
Cupcake/env-guard false positive encountered in /home/banon/projects/er-effects-rs on 2026-06-19.

Failing command condition:
- Intended action: create a temp file via Bash heredoc and run /home/banon/.local/bin/bd comment er-effects-rs-fl9 --file "$tmp" --json.
- The heredoc body included a literal evidence line documenting a previously-run runtime command with inline environment variables: AUTO_ALLOW_RUNTIME_PROBE=1 RUNTIME_REPO_DINPUT8=1 RUNTIME_MANUAL_LOAD=1 RUNTIME_WATCH_TARGET=game-man RUNTIME_TIMEOUT_SECONDS=60 RUNTIME_SKIP_FINAL_MEASURE=1 ARTIFACT_DIR=... ./.auto/runtime_probe.sh
- Cupcake paused the Bash command with: "appears to set environment variables directly" and suggested putting envs in ./.envs/named-env.env.

Why this is wrong:
- The blocked env assignments were inert text inside a single-quoted heredoc intended for a Beads comment, not an environment-setting operation to be executed.
- This forced a pivot to the file-write tool for the body before calling bd.

Suggested follow-up:
- Make the env-assignment guard quote/heredoc aware so it does not flag literal documentation inside heredocs or quoted strings, while still blocking executable inline env assignments when policy requires named env files.
